name: the-sovereign-individual
description: James Dale Davidson and Lord William Rees-Mogg's The Sovereign Individual — a futurology and economic transformation toolkit predicting the information age revolution: decline of the nation-state, rise of the sovereign individual, end of mass democracy, and the transition from industrial-age authority to network-age autonomy.
useCase: Understand the megapolitical shift from industrial to information age — how the microprocessor will undermine the nation-state and empower the sovereign individual.
knownRisks: The book was written in 1997 — some specifics are dated, though the framework remains prescient
The libertarian/anti-state bias is strong and may not appeal to all readers
The 'collapse' framing may overstate the speed of decline — governments have proven more resilient than predicted
